1→/// Helper macro to execute a system call that returns an `io::Result`.
     2→//
     3→// Macro must be defined before any modules that uses them.
     4→#[allow(unused_macros)]
     5→macro_rules! syscall {
     6→    ($fn: ident ( $($arg: expr),* $(,)* ) ) => {{
     7→        #[allow(unused_unsafe)]
     8→        let res = unsafe { libc::$fn($($arg, )*) };
     9→        if res < 0 {
    10→            Err(std::io::Error::last_os_error())
    11→        } else {
    12→            Ok(res)
    13→        }
    14→    }};
    15→}
    16→
    17→cfg_os_poll! {
    18→    #[cfg_attr(all(
    19→        not(mio_unsupported_force_poll_poll),
    20→        any(
    21→            target_os = "android",
    22→            target_os = "illumos",
    23→            target_os = "linux",
    24→            target_os = "redox",
    25→        )
    26→    ), path = "selector/epoll.rs")]
    27→    #[cfg_attr(all(
    28→        not(mio_unsupported_force_poll_poll),
    29→        any(
    30→            target_os = "dragonfly",
    31→            target_os = "freebsd",
    32→            target_os = "ios",
    33→            target_os = "macos",
    34→            target_os = "netbsd",
    35→            target_os = "openbsd",
    36→            target_os = "tvos",
    37→            target_os = "visionos",
    38→            target_os = "watchos",
    39→        )
    40→    ), path = "selector/kqueue.rs")]
    41→    #[cfg_attr(any(
    42→        mio_unsupported_force_poll_poll,
    43→        target_os = "aix",
    44→        target_os = "espidf",
    45→        target_os = "fuchsia",
    46→        target_os = "haiku",
    47→        target_os = "hermit",
    48→        target_os = "hurd",
    49→        target_os = "nto",
    50→        target_os = "solaris",
    51→        target_os = "vita",
    52→        target_os = "cygwin",
    53→    ), path = "selector/poll.rs")]
    54→    mod selector;
    55→    pub(crate) use self::selector::*;
    56→
    57→    #[cfg_attr(all(
    58→        not(mio_unsupported_force_waker_pipe),
    59→        any(
    60→            target_os = "android",
    61→            target_os = "espidf",
    62→            target_os = "fuchsia",
    63→            target_os = "hermit",
    64→            target_os = "illumos",
    65→            target_os = "linux",
    66→        )
    67→    ), path = "waker/eventfd.rs")]
    68→    #[cfg_attr(all(
    69→        not(mio_unsupported_force_waker_pipe),
    70→        not(mio_unsupported_force_poll_poll), // `kqueue(2)` based waker doesn't work with `poll(2)`.
    71→        any(
    72→            target_os = "freebsd",
    73→            target_os = "ios",
    74→            target_os = "macos",
    75→            target_os = "tvos",
    76→            target_os = "visionos",
    77→            target_os = "watchos",
    78→        )
    79→    ), path = "waker/kqueue.rs")]
    80→    #[cfg_attr(any(
    81→        // NOTE: also add to the list for the `pipe` module below.
    82→        mio_unsupported_force_waker_pipe,
    83→        all(
    84→            // `kqueue(2)` based waker doesn't work with `poll(2)`.
    85→            mio_unsupported_force_poll_poll,
    86→            any(
    87→                target_os = "freebsd",
    88→                target_os = "ios",
    89→                target_os = "macos",
    90→                target_os = "tvos",
    91→                target_os = "visionos",
    92→                target_os = "watchos",
    93→            ),
    94→        ),
    95→        target_os = "aix",
    96→        target_os = "dragonfly",
    97→        target_os = "haiku",
    98→        target_os = "hurd",
    99→        target_os = "netbsd",
   100→        target_os = "nto",
   101→        target_os = "openbsd",
   102→        target_os = "redox",
   103→        target_os = "solaris",
   104→        target_os = "vita",
   105→        target_os = "cygwin",
   106→    ), path = "waker/pipe.rs")]
   107→    mod waker;
   108→    // NOTE: the `Waker` type is expected in the selector module as the
   109→    // `poll(2)` implementation needs to do some special stuff.
   110→
   111→    #[cfg(feature = "os-ext")]
   112→    mod sourcefd;
   113→    #[cfg(feature = "os-ext")]
   114→    pub use self::sourcefd::SourceFd;
   115→
   116→    cfg_net! {
   117→        mod net;
   118→
   119→        pub(crate) mod tcp;
   120→        pub(crate) mod udp;
   121→        #[cfg(not(target_os = "hermit"))]
   122→        pub(crate) mod uds;
   123→    }
   124→
   125→    #[cfg(all(
   126→        any(
   127→            // For the public `pipe` module, must match `cfg_os_ext` macro.
   128→            feature = "os-ext",
   129→            // For the `Waker` type based on a pipe.
   130→            mio_unsupported_force_waker_pipe,
   131→            all(
   132→                // `kqueue(2)` based waker doesn't work with `poll(2)`.
   133→                mio_unsupported_force_poll_poll,
   134→                any(
   135→                    target_os = "freebsd",
   136→                    target_os = "ios",
   137→                    target_os = "macos",
   138→                    target_os = "tvos",
   139→                    target_os = "visionos",
   140→                    target_os = "watchos",
   141→                ),
   142→            ),
   143→            // NOTE: also add to the list for the `pipe` module below.
   144→            target_os = "aix",
   145→            target_os = "dragonfly",
   146→            target_os = "haiku",
   147→            target_os = "hurd",
   148→            target_os = "netbsd",
   149→            target_os = "nto",
   150→            target_os = "openbsd",
   151→            target_os = "redox",
   152→            target_os = "solaris",
   153→            target_os = "vita",
   154→            target_os = "cygwin",
   155→        ),
   156→        // Hermit doesn't support pipes.
   157→        not(target_os = "hermit"),
   158→    ))]
   159→    pub(crate) mod pipe;
   160→}
   161→
   162→cfg_not_os_poll! {
   163→    cfg_os_ext! {
   164→        mod sourcefd;
   165→        pub use self::sourcefd::SourceFd;
   166→    }
   167→}
   168→

Here's the full analysis:

---

## (1) Complete File List

| # | File |
|---|---|
| 1 | `src/sys/mod.rs` |
| 2 | `src/sys/shell/mod.rs` |
| 3 | `src/sys/shell/selector.rs` |
| 4 | `src/sys/shell/tcp.rs` |
| 5 | `src/sys/shell/udp.rs` |
| 6 | `src/sys/shell/uds.rs` |
| 7 | `src/sys/shell/waker.rs` |
| 8 | `src/sys/unix/mod.rs` |
| 9 | `src/sys/unix/net.rs` |
| 10 | `src/sys/unix/pipe.rs` |
| 11 | `src/sys/unix/selector/epoll.rs` |
| 12 | `src/sys/unix/selector/kqueue.rs` |
| 13 | `src/sys/unix/selector/poll.rs` |
| 14 | `src/sys/unix/selector/stateless_io_source.rs` |
| 15 | `src/sys/unix/sourcefd.rs` |
| 16 | `src/sys/unix/tcp.rs` |
| 17 | `src/sys/unix/udp.rs` |
| 18 | `src/sys/unix/uds/datagram.rs` |
| 19 | `src/sys/unix/uds/listener.rs` |
| 20 | `src/sys/unix/uds/mod.rs` |
| 21 | `src/sys/unix/uds/stream.rs` |
| 22 | `src/sys/unix/waker/eventfd.rs` |
| 23 | `src/sys/unix/waker/kqueue.rs` |
| 24 | `src/sys/unix/waker/pipe.rs` |
| 25 | `src/sys/wasi/mod.rs` |
| 26 | `src/sys/windows/afd.rs` |
| 27 | `src/sys/windows/event.rs` |
| 28 | `src/sys/windows/handle.rs` |
| 29 | `src/sys/windows/io_status_block.rs` |
| 30 | `src/sys/windows/iocp.rs` |
| 31 | `src/sys/windows/mod.rs` |
| 32 | `src/sys/windows/named_pipe.rs` |
| 33 | `src/sys/windows/net.rs` |
| 34 | `src/sys/windows/overlapped.rs` |
| 35 | `src/sys/windows/selector.rs` |
| 36 | `src/sys/windows/tcp.rs` |
| 37 | `src/sys/windows/udp.rs` |
| 38 | `src/sys/windows/waker.rs` |

## (2) Total Count

**38 files**

## (3) Architecture: Platform Abstraction Pattern

Mio uses a **compile-time `cfg`-gated module substitution** pattern (sometimes called the "platform facade" or "conditional re-export" pattern). Here's how it works:

### Top-level dispatcher: `src/sys/mod.rs`

The root `mod.rs` acts as a **switchboard**. It conditionally compiles *one* of four platform backends and re-exports everything with `pub use self::<platform>::*`:

```
cfg(unix | hermit) + cfg(os-poll)  →  mod unix;  pub use self::unix::*;
cfg(windows)       + cfg(os-poll)  →  mod windows; pub use self::windows::*;
cfg(wasi)          + cfg(os-poll)  →  mod wasi;  pub(crate) use self::wasi::*;
cfg(not os-poll)                   →  mod shell;  pub(crate) use self::shell::*;
```

The rest of the crate just uses `crate::sys::Selector`, `crate::sys::Waker`, `crate::sys::tcp`, etc. — it never knows which backend is behind them. This is **duck-typing at compile time**: every backend must export the same set of types/functions (`Selector`, `Waker`, `Event`, `Events`, `IoSourceState`, `tcp`, `udp`, etc.) with matching signatures, but they share **no common trait**. The contract is structural, not nominal.

### Two-tier `cfg` dispatch within Unix

The Unix backend adds a second layer of compile-time dispatch for I/O multiplexer and waker mechanism:

| Component | `cfg` selector | Implementations |
|---|---|---|
| **Selector** (I/O multiplexer) | `#[cfg_attr(..., path = "selector/X.rs")]` | `epoll.rs` (Linux, Android, illumos, Redox) · `kqueue.rs` (macOS, FreeBSD, etc.) · `poll.rs` (fallback: AIX, Haiku, Solaris, etc.) |
| **Waker** (cross-thread wake) | `#[cfg_attr(..., path = "waker/X.rs")]` | `eventfd.rs` (Linux, Android, etc.) · `kqueue.rs` (macOS, FreeBSD) · `pipe.rs` (fallback) |

This uses Rust's `#[cfg_attr(condition, path = "...")]` attribute to conditionally set the file path for `mod selector` and `mod waker`, selecting the right implementation file at compile time.

### The `shell` backend (no-op / stub)

When `os-poll` is disabled, the `shell` module provides **stub implementations** that panic with `"mio must be compiled with 'os-poll' to run"`. This lets the crate compile (e.g., for `no_std`-like environments or doc builds) without a real I/O backend.

### Summary of the pattern

| Concept | Mechanism |
|---|---|
| Platform selection | `#[cfg(target_os/target_family)]` + custom feature flags |
| Abstraction boundary | Structural (same names + signatures), not trait-based |
| Binding time | Compile-time — zero runtime dispatch overhead |
| Fallback | `shell` module provides panicking stubs |
| Sub-platform variance | Nested `#[cfg_attr(…, path)]` for selector/waker within Unix |

This is a classic pattern in Rust systems crates: **conditional module inclusion with glob re-export**, giving the appearance of a single `sys` module while each platform has a completely independent implementation.

---

## (4) Tool Calls Made (in order)

1. **`Bash`** — `kdb tree src/sys/ -L 4`
2. **`Glob`** — pattern `src/sys/**/*`
3. **`Read`** — `/Users/andjones/Documents/repos/mio/src/sys/mod.rs`
4. **`Read`** — `/Users/andjones/Documents/repos/mio/src/sys/unix/mod.rs`
5. **`Read`** — `/Users/andjones/Documents/repos/mio/src/sys/windows/mod.rs`
6. **`Read`** — `/Users/andjones/Documents/repos/mio/src/sys/shell/mod.rs`
7. **`Read`** — `/Users/andjones/Documents/repos/mio/src/sys/wasi/mod.rs`

(Calls 1–2 were parallel; calls 3–7 were parallel.)
